title: Liveblog  Article in live   Marine Le Pen, convicted but eligible to run for president, to speak tonight
subhead: An appeals court on Tuesday found the far-right leader guilty of embezzlement but shortened her ban on holding elected office, allowing her to stand in the 2027 election. However, it also sentenced her to at-home detention with an electronic tag for a year, although that term could be reduced.

title: Explosions rock Damascus during Macron's visit
Farage to resign as MP, force by-election amid financial scrutiny
How Malian jihadists and pro-independence groups joined forces to overthrow the junta
subhead: For years, jihadists from JNIM and pro-independence fighters from the FLA harbored deadly hatred for each other. But they put their differences aside to confront their common enemy: the ruling junta in Bamako. Since April 25, they have launched a major offensive.

title: French prosecutors open probe into Paraguay senator's racist abuse of Mbappé
subhead: Celeste Amarilla mocked Kylian Mbappé's origins, upbringing and appearance on X after his winning penalty knocked Paraguay out of the World Cup.

title: PFAS: Less than 2% of emissions could be removed from environment, even with massive investments
subhead: A study by scientists and journalists from the Forever Pollution Project, coordinated by Le Monde, reveals that cleanup efforts are largely ineffective and highlights the urgent need for strict limits on emissions.

title: Pyrénées wildfires destroy everything in their path: 'I didn't think I'd make it out alive'
subhead: The residents of Ille-sur-Têt, a town that was evacuated, remain in shock as the Trévillach wildfire – the largest the region has seen in 50 years – was still not under control on Monday evening.

Vladimir Putin, or the loneliness of 'Homo Sovieticus'
subhead: Between denial of reality, a desire for revenge and carefully crafted messaging, the Russian president has tried to position himself as the master of the game. But after more than four years of war in Ukraine, these old tricks inherited from the Soviet past are showing their limitations, and he is losing credibility.

title: Somaliland's high-risk quest for recognition
subhead: The self-proclaimed republic aspires to accede to the international stage. It finds itself at the center of a game of alliances, caught between Israeli, American and Emirati ambitions and threats from Yemen and neighboring Somalia.

title: Philosopher Mélanie Plouviez: 'The rich are not rich alone; they are in a social world that protects them'
subhead: Wealth is not determined solely by the possessions and assets one accumulates, philosopher Mélanie Plouviez explains in an interview with Le Monde. Since antiquity, a person's fortune has also been defined by one's social standing, relationships of dominance, dependence and honor.
